channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🌍 Iran Is Winning the Vibe War | Foreign Policy
item.description: Disinformation experts need a new framework in the era of AI slop.

item.title: 🏛️ ‘Going to change everything’: Special Forces joins Army’s next-gen C2 prototype experiments | Breaking Defense
item.description: After hearing how the 4th Infantry Division was using its new C2 tech, 10th Special Forces Group got in on the action, including at the recent Ivy Mass exercise.

item.title: 🏴 Ukraine Has a New War Strategy—and It’s Working | Foreign Policy
item.description: A year ago, the Ukrainian government decided to take the fight directly to Russia. It hasn’t looked back since.

item.title: 🌏 Marine published in the Bulletin of the Atomic Scientists on trilateral arms control | Atlantic Council
item.description: On May 13, Forward Defense Associate Director and Resident Fellow Alyxandra Marine co-authored an article in the Bulletin of the Atomic Scientists, arguing that the United States should pursue trilateral arms-control arrangements with Russia and China. While near-term agreement…

item.title: 🏛️ US soldiers learn to identify drones by sound | Breaking Defense
item.description: While the US Army has not yet formally introduced audio drone training as part of its curriculum, Harrington told reporters that field experience listening to the sky can provide an introduction to differentiating among various drone types.

item.title: ⚛️ ‘Cheap’ Patriot Interceptor Costing Under $1 Million Now Being Sought By Army | The War Zone
item.description: The Army knows it needs a more affordable and producible Patriot option as enemies seek overmatch through cheap drones and throngs of ballistic missiles. The post ‘Cheap’ Patriot Interceptor Costing Under $1 Million Now Being Sought By Army appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 A connected threat needs a connected framework | Atlantic Council
item.description: To mitigate future energy crises, the world needs more than its current patchwork approach. It needs to build a globally coordinated framework. The post A connected threat needs a connected framework appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏛️ Five companies win DoD’s Drone Dominance small drone ‘Lethality Prize Challenge’ | Breaking Defense
item.description: “[The] Lethality Challenge selection gives us a rail-locked pathway to thousands or tens of thousands of unit orders for this product, which has absolutely changed the caliber of discussion we’re having with investors, suppliers, other customers/partners, etc,” drone-maker…

item.title: 🌏 Kroenig interviewed on Fox News on Trump’s trip to China | Atlantic Council
item.description: On May 14, Atlantic Council vice president and Scowcroft Center senior director Matthew Kroenig was interviewed on Fox News radio on President Trump's trip to China. The post Kroenig interviewed on Fox News on Trump’s trip to China appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🌏 Why Did Trump Visit the Temple of Heaven in Beijing? | The Diplomat
item.description: A look back at Beijing’s choices for where to stage diplomatic scenes with U.S. presidents – and the changing political narratives.

item.title: 🏛️ Kroenig and Marine published in the Bulletin of the Atomic Scientists | Atlantic Council
item.description: On May 13, Atlantic Council vice president and Scowcroft Center senior director Matthew Kroenig and Forward Defense associate director and resident fellow Alyxandra Marine published a piece in Bulletin of the Atomic Scientists, arguing that Washington should pursue trilateral…

item.title: 🏴 Two-Seat Variant Of Russia’s Su-57 Felon Fighter Materializes | The War Zone
item.description: The apparent emergence of a two-seat Su-57 points to unmanned teaming and may also indicate a revived export drive. The post Two-Seat Variant Of Russia’s Su-57 Felon Fighter Materializes appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌏 The new Indo-Pacific security architecture: From hub-and-spoke to allied security web | Breaking Defense
item.description: This is the latest in a series of semi-regular columns by Robbin Laird, where he will tackle current defense issues through the lens of more than 45 years of defense expertise in both the US and abroad. The goal of these columns: to look back at how questions and perspectives of…

item.title: 🏴 Ukraine’s Homegrown Glide Bomb Breaks Cover | The War Zone
item.description: Ukraine is in urgent need of additional air-launched standoff munitions, especially ones free of foreign supply chains and use restrictions. The post Ukraine’s Homegrown Glide Bomb Breaks Cover appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌏 What’s on the Agenda for the 3rd Lee-Takaichi Summit? | The Diplomat
item.description: Japan and South Korea are seeking to further institutionalize their security, energy, and strategic cooperation – especially after Trump’s trip to Beijing.

item.title: 🏴 Ukrainian Agriculture as Strategy, Diplomacy, and Legacy | War on the Rocks
item.description: Welcome to The Ukraine Compass, a weekly digest of Ukrainian commentary and analysis from across the political spectrum only for War on the Rocks members. Each Monday, we bring you a curated selection of articles from Ukrainian media offering insight into how Ukrainians…

item.title: 🏛️ US Air Force looks to convert offshore oil rigs into rocket recovery platforms | Defense News
item.description: An Air Force plan calls for old oil platforms to become Sea-based Recovery Stations for the U.S. Space Force and private spaceflight companies.

item.title: 🌏 The Trump-Xi Summit’s Nuclear Shadow Over Asia | The Diplomat
item.description: Beneath the summit optics lies the reality that China’s rapid nuclear transformation is increasingly straining extended deterrence and unsettling the United States’ Asian allies.

item.title: 🌏 How Gulf investments are responding to the US-China critical minerals competition | Atlantic Council
item.description: Gulf sovereign wealth funds are seeking to maximize return on investment while maintaining strategic optionality. The post How Gulf investments are responding to the US-China critical minerals competition appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🌍 Iran Could Be Trump’s Greatest Failure | Foreign Policy
item.description: A lot of things have gone horribly wrong—and it’s not over yet.

item.title: 🌏 Taiwan’s Special Defense Budget Cut Will Cost Its Drone Capabilities | The Diplomat
item.description: The special budget passed by the Legislative Yuan ends all new domestic defense drone procurement at a critical moment in the industry's development.

item.title: 🌍 The Gulf’s African Aftershocks | Foreign Policy
item.description: How the Iran war is deepening proxy conflicts around the Red Sea and the Horn of Africa.

item.title: 🏴 At SCO Security Heads Meeting, Russia Stakes Out ‘Full-fledged Partnership’ With Taliban-ruled Afghanistan | The Diplomat
item.description: Russia's Sergei Shoigu also said that "a return of third-country military infrastructure to Afghanistan or the deployment of new military facilities in neighboring states" would be "unacceptable."

item.title: 🌏 Kim Jong Un Convenes a Military-Wide Commander Meeting | The Diplomat
item.description: The North Korean leader called for restructuring the army’s training system and reinforcing front-line units along the southern border, as Pyongyang deepens its push to redefine the security landscape of the Korean Peninsula.

item.title: 📍 Mongolia’s Long Struggle to Ensure Accountability in Governance | The Diplomat
item.description: Is a new bill the answer, or just the latest iteration of factional infighting in the ruling MPP?

item.title: 🌏 Former University of Michigan researcher accused of hiding Chinese military drone ties | Defense News
item.description: Investigators say Chuan Wang claimed modest roles on visa applications when in fact he was publicly known in China for his military drones.

item.title: 🏛️ Multiyear buys for F-35, F-15EX? Sen. Budd hopeful airpower bills added to NDAA | Breaking Defense
item.description: The North Carolina lawmaker has sponsored a trio of bills aimed at boosting fighter jet inventory and retaining pilots.

item.title: 📍 The United States: Sanctions Implementer and Sanctions Safe Haven? | Just Security
item.description: For decades, the United States has stood as the greatest leader in the sanctions space, as well as the greatest provider of tools for sanctioned entities to circumvent them. The post The United States: Sanctions Implementer and Sanctions Safe Haven? appeared first on Just…

item.title: 🌏 Trump’s China Opening and Japan’s Taiwan Anxiety | The Diplomat
item.description: Tokyo fears being sidelined again as Washington and Beijing recalibrate their relationship. Call it the “Trump Shock.”

item.title: 📍 Bridging Geopolitical Divides: Tokayev’s Message at the Antalya Diplomacy Forum | The Diplomat
item.description: Tokayev’s remarks at the Antalya Diplomacy Forum underscored Kazakhstan’s ambition to play a larger stabilizing role in global diplomacy.
